summary: This cluster examines the ecological succession and community dynamics of epilithic diatoms during the early stages of biofilm formation on artificial substrates, specifically in the Kumho River. A total of 126 diatom taxa, comprising 114 species and 12 varieties, were collected over a 70-day period in 2002, with cluster analysis revealing three distinct successional stages (early, middle, and later). The research focuses on the taxonomic composition and temporal patterns of community assembly in a freshwater lotic ecosystem, highlighting the role of species-specific colonization and turnover in shaping epilithic diatom communities.
entity_sample: Landscape-scale influences on stream habitats and biota
